tasks.title: User entity + UserRepository + repo integration test
SecurityConfig + CSRF + public allow-list
review_blockers_resolved: CSRF enforcement tests added (3) — admin POST without token → 403, cookie repo bean wired, webhook bypass verified

tasks.title: RbacService + @EnableMethodSecurity
review_blockers_resolved: AnonymousAuthenticationToken bypass — explicit instanceof check added; new unit test asserts the trap is now caught

tasks.title: AuditLogAspect AOP
review_blockers_resolved: Pointcut excluded @ExceptionHandler methods — failed login now writes exactly one audit row (was two: status=500 + status=401). Regression test added; unused imports removed.

tasks.title: Frontend API client + auth helpers (incl. lib/log.ts)
Admin layout auth guard + sidebar shell
V003 clients migration + Client entity + repo + integration test
concerns_resolved_in_plan: V001 already creates `client` table — V003 recast as comment-only marker.
Plan's Client entity spec was missing real V001 columns (preferred_channel, rodo_consent_at, citext email, nullable last_name, client_contact_present check). Entity mapped to actual schema.

Plan referenced non-existent PostgresIntegrationTestBase — actual base is AbstractIntegrationTest. Plan errata block added at top of plan file documenting V001 reality + correct base class for downstream tasks.

tasks.title: ClientService (CRUD + search) + unit test
deviations_from_plan: Added 5th unit test for client_contact_present invariant (plan had 4).
Added ClientContactMissingException + applied invariant in both create() and update().
Aligned validation with V001 schema (phone max 40 not 32; lastName not @NotBlank since column is nullable).
ClientSearchResult.fullName made null-safe for nullable lastName.

tasks.title: ClientController + RBAC + integration test
deviations_from_plan: Added spring-security-test (test scope) — wasn't on classpath before.
Used SecurityMockMvcRequestPostProcessors.user().roles() instead of plan's manually-stuffed MockHttpSession (SS6-idiomatic).

Initial commit placed ClientController at com.drshoes.app.client — outside the AuditLogAspect pointcut (.api.). Fixup relocated to com.drshoes.app.client.api and added postCreateWritesAuditRow regression test. Plan errata updated to encode the .api. controller-package convention for downstream tasks 1-7/1-10/1-11.

tasks.title: V004 orders migration + Order/OrderItem entities + repos
deviations_from_plan: V004 became ALTER TABLE order_ ADD COLUMN version (not no-op marker — V001 lacks the column required by @Version optimistic-locking architecture).
Added OrderSource enum (V001 declares source NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ADMIN'; Java needs a default to avoid NULL inserts).
Optimistic-locking conflict test deferred to task 1-6 (OrderService) — repo-layer reproduction needs a detached EntityManager.

tasks.title: OrderCodeSequence service + integration test
deviations_from_plan: Used JdbcTemplate (per errata) instead of plan-body's EntityManager approach.
Test is integration (not unit as plan title says) — tests the real PL/pgSQL function, not a mock.
Year isolation via deleting 1999/1998 rows in @BeforeEach (avoids counter pollution from sibling tests sharing the reused container).
Concurrency proven: 8 parallel CompletableFuture.runAsync calls produce 8 distinct codes.
tasks.protocol_drift: Subagent split work into two commits (service + dispatch log) — instruction was atomic. Not worth fixup; tighten prompt next time.

tasks.title: OrderService CRUD + status change + soft-delete + items
review_blockers_resolved: 3 plan-mandated tests missing (list status filter, list q= search, update on soft-deleted) — added in fixup. Production code was already correct.
Optimistic-lock test proves app-layer guard, not JPA OLE backstop — accepted (the manual version compare is the intended design).

Initial status ambiguity (PRZYJETE vs WSTEPNIE_PRZYJETE) — resolved: PRZYJETE for ADMIN-source orders (Misza creating manually = already received and confirmed). WSTEPNIE_PRZYJETE reserved for future PUBLIC_INTAKE source. Plan body wins.
deviations_from_plan: OrderService at 165 LOC / 133 effective — 13 lines over the 120 ceiling. Split into OrderService + OrderItemService + OrderSpecifications. Reviewer accepted the 'no cohesion benefit from further splitting' argument.

tasks.title: OrderController + ExceptionHandler + integration test
deviations_from_plan: GET /orders takes single ?status= (not comma-separated List<OrderStatus>) because OrderService.list shipped in 1-6 takes single status. Plan body's multi-status filter is debt against later need; design spec doesn't strictly require it.

OrderController 142 LOC — over 120 ceiling. Accepted under the CRUD-controller exception (mirrors the JPA-entity exception). No clean split: all 9 endpoints share rbac/svc dependency, splitting would just move LOC sideways.
Test @AfterEach cleanupOrders() added — FK order_.client_id forces orders to be deleted before clients in shared Testcontainers reuse mode.
tasks.audit_coverage: postCreateWritesAuditRow regression test asserts audit_log delta == 1 after POST. Controller located at com.drshoes.app.order.api.OrderController per .api. convention — pointcut matches.

tasks.title: V005 audit_parent_entity + AuditLogAspect parent-id population
review_blockers_resolved: Test 'methodWithoutAuditedAnnotationLeavesParentEntityIdNull' was vacuously true (called clientService.create() directly with no HTTP context — controller pointcut bailed, zero rows written, assertion ran over empty list). Fixed: switched to AdminWebTestBase + MockMvc POST so a real audit row gets written and asserted.

Test 'spelFailureDoesNotBreakAuditRow' didn't exercise the full proxy pipeline — only tested the resolver in isolation. Fixed: @TestConfiguration adds BrokenSpelAuditedService bean with @Audited(parent='#nonExistentVariable.toString()') (chain forces SpelEvaluationException — undefined variable alone returns null in SimpleEvaluationContext); test calls through Spring proxy, asserts method returns normally, audit row written with parent_entity_id null, WARN log captured via Logback ListAppender.
key_decisions: Two-row audit architecture: HTTP controller advice writes one row (path=/api/admin/.../, parent_entity_id=null); @Audited service advice writes a SECOND row (path=synthetic 'OrderService#addItem', method=INTERNAL, status=0, parent_entity_id=orderId). Reviewer confirmed sound — alternative thread-local enrichment was rejected as fragile.
Sandboxing: SimpleEvaluationContext.forReadOnlyDataBinding() — blocks T(...) reflection + bean refs.
Exception safety: try/catch around SpEL eval in AuditedParentResolver; WARN log; returns null; audit row still writes.
-parameters compile flag is enabled in root pom.xml (pluginManagement/maven-compiler-plugin) — required for #orderId-style param-name SpEL bindings.

tasks.title: TimelineEventCurator (pure function) + unit tests
deviations_from_plan: Plan body assumed AuditLog had entityType/operation/fieldsChanged/before-after JSON — none of those exist on the actual entity. Curator built as a path-pattern dispatcher keyed on (method, path, parentEntityId, status). Documented as M2 debt: body-capture would unlock ASSIGNEE_CHANGED + PICKUP_DATE_CHANGED + field-level deltas.

9 enum kinds shipped; only 7 emitted: ORDER_CREATED, ORDER_UPDATED (new), STATUS_CHANGED, ORDER_SOFT_DELETED, ITEM_ADDED, ITEM_EDITED, ITEM_REMOVED. ASSIGNEE_CHANGED + PICKUP_DATE_CHANGED reserved for M2 (in enum, never dispatched). ORDER_UPDATED is the generic PATCH event since the curator can't see field-level info.
HTTP item-op rows (POST /orders/{id}/items etc) are intentionally SKIPPED — the INTERNAL @Audited service row covers them. Prevents double-count. Test covers this.
AuditLog needed 4 missing getters (getMethod, getPath, getStatus, getActorId) — added inline. Mechanical accessor additions, not behavioral changes.
TimelineEventCurator 140 LOC = 90 logic + 50 Javadoc. Over 120 ceiling but documentation is load-bearing (URL-prefix brittleness debt is documented in class Javadoc).

tasks.title: AuditTimelineService + Controller + integration test
deviations_from_plan: Plan body referenced AuditLog.entity_type / entity_id columns (don't exist). Repo query rewritten to scan `path LIKE :pathPrefix OR parent_entity_id = :orderId ORDER BY createdAt`. Two-parameter method signature: caller (service) constructs pathPrefix.

ORDER_CREATED event SYNTHESIZED from Order.createdAt — the POST /api/admin/orders audit row has no orderId in its path, so the LIKE scan can't find it. Actor resolved from the earliest scanned row. If order not found (unknown orderId), empty list returned. M2 debt: TimelineEvent.id is null for the synthetic event; frontend must handle.
AuditTimelineController placed at com.drshoes.app.audit.api.AuditTimelineController per .api. convention.
RBAC: no @PreAuthorize — SecurityConfig filter chain already enforces isAuthenticated() on /api/admin/**. Test 3 proves anonymous → 401.

tasks.title: UsersController for assignee dropdown
deviations_from_plan: UserStubDto placed at com.drshoes.app.auth.api.dto.UserStubDto (matches existing 0B layout) rather than com.drshoes.app.auth.dto.* as plan body suggests.
User entity uses `active` boolean (not `deletedAt`) — repo method named findAllByActiveTrueOrderByFullName.
User.getFullName() already returns a single-column persisted value — no null-safe concat needed.
Audit-coverage regression test intentionally omitted — endpoint is read-only GET, aspect only fires on writes.

tasks.title: lib/clients + lib/orders + lib/timeline modules
deviations_from_plan: lib/orders/types.ts is 154 LOC — over the 120 ceiling for TS modules. Pure type declarations, accepted.

Frontend ClientDto types include preferredChannel? + rodoConsentAt? as optional. The backend ClientDto.java OMITS these fields even though they exist on the Client entity — backend debt to file separately. Frontend papered over with optional typing so wire absence doesn't break consumers.
Tailwind tokens (acid/magenta/blue/orange/green/admin-line/admin-mute) confirmed live in packages/ui/src/tokens.ts via preset; no stock fallbacks needed.
Endpoint paths all matched plan body; orders list uses ?type= (repeatable) for item kinds.
follow_up_debts: Backend ClientDto.java should expose preferredChannel + rodoConsentAt — file as separate task before M1 closes if admin UI needs to edit these fields, otherwise defer to M2 messaging milestone.

tasks.title: ClientPicker + ClientCreateModal components
deviations_from_plan: ClientPicker split per plan into ClientPicker.tsx (101 LOC) + ClientPickerResults.tsx (51 LOC). Picker over 80 LOC soft cap but under 120; modal also 96 LOC.
Keyboard nav (arrow up/down + Enter) deferred — mouse-only for M1. Documented.
onMouseDown+preventDefault used on result rows to prevent onBlur from closing dropdown before click — standard combobox technique not in plan.

tasks.title: Orders list page + filters + table
deviations_from_plan: Plan body said multi-checkbox status filter (6 statuses) — WRONG. Backend OrderController takes single ?status=. Implemented as single <select> with 'Wszystkie' empty option. M1 debt.
Cookie-forwarding pattern from lib/auth/session.ts mirrored in new lib/orders/api-server.ts (server-only module).
OrderListRow DTO is missing 'items' array — 'Pozycje' column falls back to description. Backend debt.
OrderListRow DTO is missing 'assignedCraftsmanId' — 'Wykonawca' column renders '—'. Backend debt.
/admin/orders/new Link uses `as any` cast — typed routes won't know the path until 1-15 ships. Resolves automatically next task.
follow_up_debts: Backend OrderListRow.java should include `items` (or at least an items-count + kinds summary) and `assignedCraftsmanId` fields. File as separate task before M1 closure if assignee + items columns matter for UAT, otherwise defer.

tasks.title: Create-new-order page
deviations_from_plan: Items inline-create included — CreateOrderRequest.java accepts List<CreateOrderItemRequest> items.
Form split into NewOrderForm.tsx (116 LOC) + NewOrderItemRow.tsx (62 LOC) to stay under granular-code ceiling.
Both `as any` casts on /admin/orders/new Link in 1-14's OrdersTable removed — typed routes now resolve naturally.
receivedAt field on CreateOrderRequest exists but not wired in the form — backend defaults to now(). M1 acceptable; could add to drawer later.

review_blockers_resolved: AdminPrincipalLoginTest missed the audit_log FK teardown that the other three modified tests received — added auditLogs.deleteAll() before users.deleteAll() in seed() and cleanup(), suite stays 153/0/0/1.
review_followups_deferred: AuthController.me() still does a DB lookup for fullName + lastLoginAt because those fields are not on AdminPrincipal. Optional follow-up: fold them onto the principal to eliminate the lookup. Behavior is correct as-is.

tasks.title: PhotoService + @Audited + TimelineEventCurator extension (TWO-STAGE)
review_blockers_resolved: Cross-order photo ID enumeration — added orderId param + verifyOwnership() guard to relabel/delete/stream; cross-order calls throw PhotoNotFoundException (info-leak resistant). 3 new ownership-guard tests in PhotoServiceTest.
stream() pinned a JDBC connection for the full download — dropped @Transactional(readOnly=true); findById opens its own short tx, blob InputStream is held by caller without spanning the JPA session.
review_followups_deferred: MIME magic-byte verification — service trusts multipart Content-Type header (admin-only surface; low risk). Track for task 3-7 if Apache Tika or similar is added.
PhotoService is 232 LOC vs 120 cap — cohesive, no clean split point. Acknowledged in dispatch log.

notes: Tasks 0b-1 and 0b-2 shipped before the dispatch-log directive, so dispatch_log is null. Future tasks must include a dispatch_log path.
Per locked dispatch protocol: plans on disk only; thin prompts; combined reviews for mechanical tasks; structured logging baked in from 0b-3 onward.

Hardening debt (from 0b-4 review, but pre-existing in Task 2): server.servlet.session.cookie.secure is set to false in application.yaml — contradicts ARCHITECTURE.md which mandates Secure on session cookie. Fix in a focused config-hardening pass that introduces an env-aware profile (local=false, prod=true), or set true and rely on forward-headers-strategy=framework everywhere. Track before milestone-0b tag.

PII flag (from 0b-5 inline review): LoginThrottle logs raw client IP at INFO on throttle events. Polish RODO/GDPR treats IP as personal data. Acceptable for now (security event); revisit during AuditLogAspect (0b-8) to apply a unified PII-redaction or hashing policy across security-event logs.
